title: Concordance Engine Packet Schema (v1.0.5 — engine-aligned)
description: Describes the packet shape the engine actually validates as of v1.0.5. All <DOMAIN>_VERIFY blocks are extended this release; see field descriptions for the recognized sub-fields. The original long-form schema is preserved at packet.schema.aspirational.json.

domain.description: Routes the packet to a domain validator. Unknown domains pass with a 'no validator registered' note.

id.description: Optional packet identifier. Not enforced by the engine.

created_epoch.description: Unix epoch seconds when the packet was created. Required for the GOD gate; missing or zero quarantines.

required_witnesses.description: BROTHERS-gate threshold. 0 disables the witness check.

witness_count.description: Affirming witnesses present at evaluation time.

text.description: Plain-text decision description. Used by the governance/business/household/education/church validators.

description.description: Alias for 'text' accepted by the governance validator.

claims.description: Fallback claims list used when domain-specific RED/FLOOR blocks are absent. Items may be strings or {subject, predicate} dicts.

artifacts.description: Fallback artifacts map used when domain-specific FLOOR blocks are absent.

diagnostics.description: Diagnostic chain entries consumed by domain validators (chemistry, CS, statistics).

MATH_VERIFY.description: Computational verification artifacts for mathematics. Recognized fields: expr_a/expr_b/variables (symbolic equality); function/variable/claimed_derivative; integrand/variable/claimed_antiderivative; function/variable/point/claimed_limit; equation/variable/claimed_solutions; matrix/claim_type (rank|determinant|trace|eigenvalues|transpose|inverse|symmetric|invertible|nullspace_dim)/claimed_value; lhs/rhs/op/variable for inequality; term/start/end/claimed_sum for series; ode/claimed_solution/variable/function for ODE.

PHYS_VERIFY.description: Computational verification artifacts for physics. Recognized fields: equation/symbols (dimensional consistency); before/after/tolerance_relative/tolerance_absolute (per-quantity conservation); law (energy|momentum|charge|mass) plus before/after invokes named-law profile checking (KE+PE total energy, vector momentum components, scalar charge/mass).

units.description: Unit system declaration (e.g. 'SI'). Satisfies physics FLOOR.

CHEM_VERIFY.description: Computational verification artifacts for chemistry. Recognized fields: equation (atom/charge balance); temperature_K (positive absolute temperature).

BIO_VERIFY.description: Computational verification artifacts for biology. Recognized fields: n_replicates/min_replicates; assay_classes/min_assay_classes; dose_response (doses, responses, expected_direction); power_analysis (effect_size, alpha, n_per_group, target_power); hardy_weinberg.counts (3-element AA/Aa/aa list, chi-squared vs HWE expectation); primer.sequence (length/GC%/Wallace Tm sanity), optional gc_range and tm_range; molarity.mass_g+mw_g_per_mol+volume_L+claimed_molarity (or moles+volume_L); mendelian.observed+expected_ratio (chi-squared).

CS_VERIFY.description: Computational verification artifacts for computer science. Recognized fields: code (Python source); function_name; test_cases; input_generator; claimed_class; claimed_space_class; sizes; tolerance; trials (>=2 enables run-twice determinism check).

STAT_VERIFY.description: Computational verification artifacts for statistics. Recognized fields: test (one of: two_sample_t, one_sample_t, paired_t, z, chi2, f, one_proportion_z, two_proportion_z, fisher_exact, mannwhitney, wilcoxon_signed_rank, regression_coefficient_t); test inputs (n, mean, sd, df, statistic, table, etc.); tail (two|two-sided|greater|less); claimed_p; tolerance. Multiple-comparisons: raw_p_values/method/alpha/claimed_rejected_indices. Confidence interval: estimate/ci_low/ci_high (shape check) plus optional mean/sd/n/conf_level (bound recomputation).

DECISION_PACKET.description: Structured governance / business / household / education / church decision packet. Verifier checks structural completeness (title, scope, red_items, floor_items, way_path, execution_steps, witnesses) and witness_count consistency. Per-domain profiles add: business -> officers, fiduciary_basis (recommended: dollar_amount, risk_assessment); household -> budget_category, affected_dependents (recommended: time_horizon, alternatives_considered); education -> affected_cohort, learning_objective (recommended: accommodation_plan, policy_reference); church -> elder_signoff, scripture_anchor (recommended: congregation_impact, prayer_record). Content judgement remains human.
